title: Governance, leadership and accountability
description: A governing body or senior management establishes oversight of, and remains accountable for, AI/ICT outcomes; responsibilities are defined and cannot be delegated away to a model, processor or third-party vendor.

title: Risk management framework
description: A documented risk-management process identifies, assesses and treats risks across the system lifecycle, applied iteratively and kept up to date.

spine.reference: Clause 6.1 & 8.2 — AI risk assessment and treatment

title: Data governance and quality
description: Data used by the system is governed for quality, accuracy, completeness and representativeness, with examination of bias and gaps relative to the intended purpose.

spine.reference: Annex A — Data for AI systems

title: Technical documentation and event logging
description: Technical documentation is drawn up and kept current, and the system records events (logs) over its lifetime to ensure traceability of functioning.

title: Human oversight
description: Natural persons can effectively oversee the system during use — monitoring operation, correctly interpreting output, and intervening in or interrupting the system.

title: Accuracy, robustness and resilience testing
description: The system achieves appropriate accuracy and robustness and performs consistently, evidenced by a programme of resilience/operational testing across its lifecycle.

title: Information security safeguards
description: Reasonable technical and organisational security measures (encryption, access control, logging and monitoring, backups) protect data and systems from compromise.

spine.reference: Annex A — Security of AI systems

title: Incident and breach management and notification
description: Incidents and personal-data breaches are detected, managed and recorded, and notified to the relevant authority and affected parties within the prescribed timelines.

title: Notice and consent
description: A lawful basis for processing is established through itemised notice and free, specific, informed, unambiguous and withdrawable consent, supported by accessible consent-management.

spine.reference: Annex A — Privacy and data-subject interests

title: Data-principal rights
description: Individuals can exercise rights over their personal data, including obtaining a summary of data processed and the parties it has been shared with.

title: Transparency and explainability
description: The system is sufficiently transparent and explainable that its outputs can be interpreted, used appropriately, and contested — accompanied by instructions for use.

title: Protection of children's data
description: Verifiable parental/guardian consent is obtained before processing a child's data, and tracking, behavioural monitoring and targeted advertising directed at children are prohibited.

spine.reference: Annex A — Impact on individuals and vulnerable groups

title: Third-party and supply-chain risk management
description: Risk arising from third-party (ICT/AI) service providers is managed as an integral part of risk management, with the entity remaining responsible for compliance.

spine.reference: Annex A — Third-party and supplier relationships

title: Customer due diligence (KYC)
description: Clients and beneficial owners are identified and verified at the commencement of a relationship using officially valid documents, on a risk-sensitive basis.

title: Transaction records and regulatory reporting
description: Prescribed transaction records are maintained and suspicious/cash transaction reports are furnished to the financial intelligence unit within the prescribed timelines.
